name: Ubiquitin-Proteasome Degradation
description: Ubiquitin-proteasome system: an E1-E2-E3 enzyme cascade builds a K48-linked polyubiquitin chain on a substrate, which the 26S proteasome recognizes, deubiquitinates, unfolds, and degrades into peptides.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Substrate with degron"]
    N2["E1 activates ubiquitin (ATP)"]
    N3["E2 carries ubiquitin"]
    N4["E3 ligase binds substrate"]
    N5["Transfer Ub to substrate"]
    N6{"K48 chain length >= 4?"}
    N7["26S proteasome binds polyUb"]
    N8["DUBs recycle ubiquitin"]
    N9["ATPase unfolds substrate"]
    N10["20S core proteolysis"]
    N11["Short peptides released"]

    N1 --> N4
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N5
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|extend| N3
    N6 -->|yes| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

nodeDetails.label: Substrate with degron
nodeDetails.detail: Target protein bearing a degradation signal

nodeDetails.label: E1 activates ubiquitin (ATP)
nodeDetails.detail: E1 forms a thioester-linked ubiquitin using ATP

nodeDetails.label: E2 carries ubiquitin
nodeDetails.detail: Ubiquitin transferred to an E2 conjugating enzyme

nodeDetails.label: Transfer Ub to substrate
nodeDetails.detail: E3 catalyzes ubiquitin transfer onto the substrate

nodeDetails.label: K48 chain length >= 4?
nodeDetails.detail: A K48-linked tetra-ubiquitin chain is the degradation signal

nodeDetails.label: ATPase unfolds substrate
nodeDetails.detail: AAA-ATPases unfold and translocate the substrate

sources.title: The ubiquitin-proteasome proteolytic pathway: destruction for the sake of construction
